data.title: Rivian unveils the new R2 electric SUV as it looks to scale up
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (RIVN) formally introduced its R2 mid-size electric SUV on Thursday.

The electric vehicle maker said R2 deliveries will start this spring, beginning with the R2 Performance with Launch Package (starting at $57,990), followed by additional premium configurations in late 2026 and standard configurations in 2027 with a starting price of $46,495. The R2 lineup will offer up to 656 horsepower, 0–60 mph in as quick as 3.6 seconds, while offering a Rivian-estimated range of up to 345 miles. Standard features will include a native NACS charging port and Autonomy+ hardware.

"R2 embodies so many of our learnings that we have accumulated—the passion and attention to detail from our team is visible everywhere," highlighted Rivian (RIVN) CEO RJ Scaringe.

Benchmark Capital sees the R2 launch as a positive for Rivian (RIVN). "We view the event as an important demand signal and proof point for manufacturing execution," updated analyst Mickey Legg. 

In Car and Driver's review of the R2, the model was noted to have more rear-seat space than rivals such as the Ford (F) Mustang Mach-E and the Tesla (TSLA) Model Y. The model was also said to have reasonable off-roading abilities.

Rivian (RIVN) plans to show off the model at the SXSW festival in Austin, Texas, today.

Shares of Rivian (RIVN) were down 3.5% in late morning trading.

data.title: RWE plans $20B push for its first U.S. gas plants to meet data center demand
data.description:
RWE (RWEOY) +3.8% in European trading Thursday after saying it expects to invest around half of its €35B ($40.4B) spending plan over the next five years on U.S. power assets, including new gas-fired power plants, as it seeks to capitalize on growing energy demand from data centers.

The German power producer said it will focus on 15 gas generation projects in Texas, Arizona and parts of the Midwest, while also further expanding its portfolio of wind and solar power, as well as battery storage facilities, according to its full-year results.

"The USA remains our most important growth market, not least due to the rapidly growing demand for electricity," RWE (RWEOY) CEO Markus Krebber said. "We are further strengthening our position with the addition of flexible gas-fired power plants."

RWE (RWEOY) also said it plans to spend €9B by 2031 in gas plants and batteries in its home market Germany, as well as €7B in onshore and solar in European core markets and Australia, plus €2B in offshore wind.

The company said it expects the spending plan to drive average adjusted earnings per share growth of 12% annually over the next five years, with adjusted earnings rising to €4.40 in 2031 from €2.48 in 2025, and translating into 10% annual increases in its dividend, which was €1.20 for 2025.

U.S. spending would take its installed capacity in the country to 22 GW by 2031 from 13 GW currently, the company said.

For 2025, RWE (RWEOY) said ⁠full-year results showed core profit fell 10% to €5.1B but still topped the €4.9B analyst consensus in a company-provided poll.

data.title: AI, chip stocks tumble after Iran war causes 'largest supply disruption' in global oil market
data.description:
Chip and AI-related stocks were largely in the red on Thursday as oil prices continued to surge amid the escalating U.S.-Israel-Iran war nearing two weeks.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) fell around 1.4%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) declined about 1.2%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) fell nearly 1.3%.

On Thursday, the International Energy Agency said that the war in the Middle East is creating the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.

Brent Futures (CO1:COM) jumped about 9%, while Crude Oil Futures (CL1:COM) surged nearly 9.6% on Thursday. The U.S. Navy is “not ready” to escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, but “quite likely” will begin to do so by the end of the month, Secretary of Energy Chris Wright said Thursday.

Global markets are “struggling to price where the risks are” amid the ongoing war, according to Nick Nelson, head of global equity strategy at Absolute Strategy Research.

Shares of AI chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) fell about 2%, while Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) slumped nearly 3%. Broadcom (AVGO) declined roughly 2%, while Qualcomm (QCOM) dipped about 1%.

Several other AI and networking-related stocks were largely in the red on Thursday. Lumentum (LITE) tumbled around 6%, while Coherent (COHR) slumped nearly 3%. Ciena (CIEN), Celestica (CLS), and Arista Networks (ANET) each fell about 2%. Cisco (CSCO) dipped roughly 1%.

Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) and GlobalFoundries (GFS) each slumped about 6%, while Micron Technology (MU) and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) each tumbled around 5%. Arm (ARM), Analog Devices (ADI), and Intel (INTC) each fell nearly 4%. Texas Instruments (TXN) declined about 3%, while Marvell Technology (MRVL) fell nearly 2%.

 Chip equipment makers: Lam Research (LRCX) tumbled around 5%, while Applied Materials (AMAT) and KLA (KLAC) each slumped about 4%. ASML (ASML) fell nearly 3%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Microsoft paves path to 'medical superintelligence' with Copilot Health
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) is expanding its AI-powered consumer health offerings with the introduction of Copilot Health, which draws data from an array of sources, including wearable devices, health records, and lab test results, to provide what it calls medical intelligence.

It believes this new feature will help pave the way for "medical superintelligence," which Microsoft defines as "health AI that can ultimately combine the wide-ranging knowledge of a general physician with the depth of a specialist."

Copilot Health will be made available through a gradual rollout process. It will initially launch in English in the U.S. for those 18 and older. Those interested in early access can join a waitlist.

It gathers data such as activity levels, sleep patterns, and vital signs from more than 50 wearable devices such as Fitbit, Oura, and Apple (AAPL) Health. It can also incorporate health records, such as visit summaries, medication lists, and test results, from more than 50,000 U.S. hospitals and provider organizations through HealthEx. Copilot Health can pull in lab results from Function as well.

Microsoft said Copilot Health conversations and data are isolated from general Copilot and will not be used for training AI models. It has stricter access, privacy, and safety controls, including encryption, and gives users the ability to manage and delete data. 

Although Apple has been at the forefront of collecting health data and providing insights for consumers, many others have entered the field. Early this year, OpenAI (OPENAI) released ChatGPT Health, which was aimed at helping users "feel more informed, prepared, and confident."

Earlier this month, Amazon (AMZN) launched its health AI agent on its website and app. The agentic AI solution is designed to provide personalized health information and guidance to patients based on lab results and other medical records accessed through the Health Information Exchange with consent.

data.title: AMD likely has more gigawatt deals in the pipeline, CPU demand has accelerated: BNP
data.description:
AMD (AMD) was in focus on Thursday after BNP Paribas held a meeting with executives and said it sees the potential for more gigawatt deals in the semiconductor company's pipeline.

“Overall [a] quite positive mood and messaging across the AI GPU and server CPU businesses, where demand continues to outstrip supply and where Agentic AI is driving a new leg of growth for CPU servers,” analyst David O'Connor wrote in a note to clients. O'Connor has an Outperform rating and a $265 price target on AMD.

Delving deeper, O'Connor said the gigawatt deals that AMD has done (like those with OpenAI (OPENAI) and Meta (META)) are likely, but it's unlikely they will include warrants. “[Company] reinforced the message that the bar is very high for warrants and pointed to the significant ecosystem work that OpenAI and Meta undertook on AMD hardware/software to justify a warrant option,” O'Connor added.

In addition, AMD said that hyperscalers are using agentic AI applications to drive high CPU utilization and thus looking to increase capacity “meaningfully.” As such, AMD said it feels good about growing its enterprise server CPU business and also mentioned that enterprise server CPU deals tend to come with incremental cloud business as well.

“We see Agentic AI as a strong driver of AMD’s CPU server business more from H2’26, given the server leadtimes of 4-5 months,” O'Connor explained.

Other takeaways included the belief that AMD has a competitive advantage in being able to customize its chips for AI workloads, depending upon customer requirements; AMD has a roadmap for co-packaged optics, but there is the belief that a couple of technologies will need to mature first before it really ramps up.

Lastly, given how notable the push in agentic AI has been, AMD has seen server CPU demand accelerate since its November analyst day, O'Connor said.

data.title: Wright says U.S. Navy not ready to escort tankers through Strait of Hormuz
data.description:
Energy Secretary Chris Wright said Thursday that the U.S. Navy is not ready yet to escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, according to CNBC.

“It’ll happen relatively soon, but it can’t happen now,” Wright told CNBC. “We’re simply not ready. All of our military assets right now are focused on destroying Iran’s offensive capabilities and the manufacturing industry that supplies their offensive capabilities.”

Wright added that the navy may be ready to escort tankers through the strait by the end of the month. 

On Tuesday, Wright posted on X that the navy had begun conducting escorts, but quickly deleted it. The White House confirmed later that day that the navy had not escorted any tankers through the strait.

data.title: Aveanna to buy Family First Homecare for $176M
data.description:
Aveanna Healthcare (AVAH) on Thursday announced an agreement to acquire Family First Homecare, a pediatric home care provider, for $175.5M in a bid to expand its geographic footprint in specialized care.

Atlanta, Georgia-based Aveanna (AVAH) disclosed in a regulatory filing that it reached an equity purchase agreement to buy all issued and outstanding membership interests of Family First in an all-cash transaction.

The home healthcare provider added that it will fund the acquisition with a combination of cash on hand and debt.

Operating 27 locations across seven states, including Florida, Family First mainly delivers private duty nursing services targeting the pediatric population.

“Family First Homecare is a tremendous cultural fit for us and reinforces our strategic mission to deliver high-quality care while bringing unprecedented value and clinical innovation to our payors and stakeholders,” Aveanna (AVAH) CEO Jeff Shaner remarked. The transaction is expected to close in Q2 2026.

data.title: Montauk Renewables outlines $175M–$190M RNG revenue target for 2026 with new Turkey, NC facility coming online
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Montauk Renewables (MNTK) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Sean McClain, President and CEO, reported growth in RNG production for 2025, stating, "despite the sale of one of our RNG facilities in 2024, we achieved growth in our 2025 RNG production." He highlighted the Pico project's expanded digestion capacity, which averaged approximately 458,000 gallons per day, 17% above contractual minimums, and noted a 31.8% increase in production from the redesigned facility compared to the prior year.
  • McClain announced the commissioning of a second RNG processing facility at the Apex landfill, which contributed to a 7.8% increase in RNG production for 2025. He discussed the GreenWave Energy Partners joint venture, which generated 706,000 RINs and $1.5 million in income for Montauk in 2025.
  • The company has begun commissioning the Turkey, North Carolina facility, expecting first-phase capital investment of approximately $200 million and production/revenue generation activities to commence in April 2026. Long-term agreements with over 40 farming locations were secured to support feedstock needs.
  • McClain detailed the completion of a $200 million senior credit facility with HASI to restructure existing debt and fund growth initiatives. He also announced a 5-year gas rights extension for the Raeger RNG facility.
  • CFO Kevin Van Asdalan stated, "Total revenues in 2025 were $176.4 million, flat compared to $175.7 million in 2024." He noted that the average realized RIN price fell to $2.33, a 29% decrease from $3.28 the previous year, while natural gas index prices increased by 51.1%. He further reported, "Net income for 2025 was $1.7 million, a decrease of $8 million or 84.5% compared to $9.7 million in 2024."

Outlook

  • McClain provided a 2026 outlook, stating, "We expect our RNG production volumes to range between 5.8 million and 6.1 million MMBtu and corresponding RNG revenues to range between $175 million and $190 million. We expect renewable electricity production volumes to range between 195,000 and 207,000 megawatt hours and corresponding renewable electricity revenues to range between $35 million and $41 million."
  • Management indicated that these guidance ranges include internal assumptions that may or may not align with current market trends.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the outlook for 2026 now features expanded production and revenue ranges, with specific inclusion of the Turkey, North Carolina project.

Financial Results

  • Revenues in 2025 were $176.4 million, compared to $175.7 million in 2024. RNG operating profit for 2025 was $38.2 million, a decrease of $17.8 million compared to $56 million in 2024. Operating profit was $0.9 million, down from $16.1 million in 2024. Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 was $35.6 million, compared to $42.6 million in 2024. Total general and administrative expenses were $31.7 million, down from $36.3 million in 2024.
  • The company produced approximately 5.6 million MMBtu of RNG in 2025, matching 2024 levels, and 177,000 megawatt hours of renewable electricity, down from 186,000 in 2024.
  • Capital expenditures were $116.5 million, up from $62.3 million in 2024, with $81 million dedicated to Montauk Ag Renewables.
  • The company completed refinancing with a new $200 million senior credit facility bearing a fixed interest rate of 10.25% and maturing in 2031, with $155 million outstanding as of March 11, 2026.

Q&A

  • Y. Zhang, Scotiabank: Asked about what drives the 2026 RNG production outlook and if additional volume is expected from 15-liter engines. Van Asdalan responded, "Generally, across our portfolio, we're seeing increases across all of our RNG sites related to our expectations of landfill improvements in our existing wellfield automation initiatives."
  • Timothy Michael Moore, Clear Street: Inquired about adjusted EBITDA growth potential relative to revenue growth, referencing capital expenditures and maintenance trends. Van Asdalan explained, "With the commissioning of our North Carolina Turkey project... there will be a significant uplift in EBITDA coming from that location," noting that certain nonrecurring costs from 2025 will not repeat in 2026.
  • Ryan Pfingst, B. Riley Securities: Asked if the $15 million RNG revenue range primarily reflects potential RIN price outcomes. Van Asdalan replied, "At the beginning of the year, we're trying to cover off various expectations, not just from our production. But to your point, a potential range of RIN pricing."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on the sustainability of growth, EBITDA inflection, and RIN price volatility. The tone was neutral to slightly positive, with analysts probing for clarity on production sources and margin upside.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, especially regarding the Turkey, North Carolina facility and the impact of nonrecurring 2025 costs. McClain stated, "definitively, you'll see an uptick in cash flows because a number of the initiatives... are capitalized as opposed to embedded in your G&A and your operating expense."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management sustained a positive outlook, while analyst tone remained probing but less skeptical, focusing on forward drivers rather than remediation of past issues.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter introduced a wider guidance range for 2026, now incorporating the Turkey, North Carolina project, whereas the previous quarter’s guidance focused on maintaining existing production and revenue levels.
  • Capital expenditure guidance for development projects increased, with more detailed breakdowns and a new credit facility to fund expansion.
  • Management’s confidence level increased, highlighting the commissioning of new facilities and the anticipated impact on EBITDA and cash flow.
  • Analysts shifted focus from maintenance spending normalization to drivers of upcoming production and revenue growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management emphasized exposure to RIN price volatility, noting, "Our profitability is highly dependent on the market price of environmental attributes, including the market price for RINs."
  • The company acknowledged cost pressures in operating and maintenance expenses, including environmental attribute expenses not present in 2024.
  • Regulatory risk was highlighted with recent developments in North Carolina swine REC requirements and responses to regulatory motions.
  • Capital intensity and debt service from the new credit facility were noted, as well as the need for future project-based financing.

Final Takeaway

Montauk Renewables management anticipates a notable uplift in both production volumes and revenues for 2026, driven by portfolio-wide improvements and the commissioning of the Turkey, North Carolina facility. With a newly secured $200 million credit facility, the company is positioned to pursue further growth initiatives and expects a return to higher EBITDA and cash flows as one-time 2025 expenses subside. Management remains attentive to market and regulatory risks, while targeting $175 million to $190 million in RNG revenues for 2026, supported by expanded operational capacity and strategic partnerships.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Sleep Number outlines double-digit second-half sales growth and product reset amid turnaround strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sleep Number Corporation (SNBR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Linda Findley, President and CEO, introduced Amy O'Keefe as the new CFO, emphasizing her experience in operational and financial transformations and her focus on streamlining business operations and strengthening the capital structure to support the turnaround strategy.
  • Findley detailed that "2025 was a pivotal year for Sleep Number as our reshaped team drove big turnaround changes at every level," highlighting full year net sales of $1.41 billion in line with previous guidance, adjusted EBITDA of $78 million exceeding guidance, and a use of cash of $18 million compared to the $50 million guidance. She reported a pro forma adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 9% and confirmed that more than $185 million of annualized costs had been removed, with an additional $50 million in fixed costs targeted for reduction in 2026.
  • Findley stated, "We launched our first new bed and a new adjustable base in January and the response from customers has been fantastic. The ComfortMode mattress priced under $1,600 gives us access to a new group of customers while maintaining personalized comfort as the core of the experience. As of the end of February, sales are 3.5x what we expected and nearly twice all the sales of all 3 C Series beds that this bed replaces." She also noted strong attach rates for adjustable bases and bedding, and a significant simplification of the product lineup from 12 mattresses to 7, organized into 3 clear collections.
  • Findley addressed liquidity and capital structure challenges, noting the hiring of Guggenheim Securities to evaluate inbound interest and advise on refinancing the credit facility.
  • Amy O'Keefe, CFO, stated, "Net sales were $347 million in Q4 or 8% below the same period in the prior year... Gross profit margin was 55.6% in the quarter, a 430 basis point decline versus the prior year, primarily driven by a $9.6 million nonrecurring inventory obsolescence charge associated with our new product launch and the impact of unit deleverage and higher tariffs. Excluding the impact of the inventory charge, adjusted gross profit margin was 58.4%." She highlighted operating expenses in the quarter at $197 million, down 9% year-over-year, and annualized cost savings initiatives of approximately $185 million.

Outlook

  • Management is not providing formal guidance for 2026 due to the magnitude of ongoing changes but indicated that Q1 net sales are expected to decline in the high teens because of early softness. However, with the full impact of the new product launch in Q2 and increased media spend, a significant improvement in year-over-year revenue performance is expected in Q2.
  • O'Keefe projected, "We further expect double-digit sales growth in the second half with the full benefit of; one, new products; two, new creative assets; and three, marketing reach with our new strategic partner, Travis Kelce. As a result of cost savings initiatives and the expected ARU improvement from new products, adjusted EBITDA for the full year is expected to increase in the high teens to mid-20s percent range year-over-year, and we expect free cash flow to be positive."

Financial Results

  • Q4 net sales were $347 million. Full year net sales reached $1.41 billion, consistent with expectations. Adjusted EBITDA for the full year was $78 million, above the most recent outlook of $70 million. Full year pro forma adjusted EBITDA margin was approximately 9%, improving by 200 basis points versus the prior year.
  • Gross profit margin for Q4 was 55.6%, with an adjusted margin of 58.4% excluding a $9.6 million inventory charge. Operating expenses for the full year were $824 million, a $136 million reduction from the prior year.
  • The company ended the year with $58 million in total liquidity and was in compliance with its credit agreement and debt covenants. Full year free cash flow was a use of $18 million, a $30 million improvement over expectations but $21 million less favorable than the prior year.

Q&A

  • Daniel Silverstein, UBS: Asked about pain points addressed with the new product line and the margin impact. Findley responded that the new lineup targets comfort, value, and durability, expanding access to younger demographics, and noted, "our ComfortMode bed is as margin accretive as our Climate360 beds... allows us to serve the customer more clearly, more directly and also protect our margins at the same time." O'Keefe added, "If I just look at the ComfortMode bed compared to the 2 beds in the C Series that it's replacing, it's a 10 percentage point gross margin improvement compared to the prior year."
  • Robert Griffin, Raymond James: Inquired about the phasing of the new product launch and EBITDA comparisons. Findley confirmed all four new beds and a new base would be available for purchase starting March 23, with most stores set by mid-April. O'Keefe clarified that the projected EBITDA improvement is based on the reported $78 million for 2025.
  • Unknown Analyst, Piper Sandler: Asked about 2026 marketing spend. Findley stated, "you'll see marketing held flat as total spend in 2026 over 2025," with Q1 spend down and Q2-Q4 up year-over-year. O'Keefe noted Q2 will see a higher increase, with back half roughly flat.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on product launch execution, margin improvements, cost savings, and liquidity management, displaying a neutral to slightly positive tone as they probed for details on operational execution and financial outlook.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing turnaround progress and product success, while acknowledging ongoing challenges and risks. Findley repeatedly highlighted strategic execution and the potential for growth with the new product line.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management shifted from a more cautious and defensive posture to a more optimistic and forward-looking stance, supported by early successes from the product reset and evidence of cost discipline.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw the formal introduction of a new CFO and a more detailed product and marketing strategy rollout, compared to the previous quarter's focus on resetting the business and securing financial flexibility.
  • Management moved from discussing upcoming product launches and marketing resets to reporting concrete results and customer response. The simplification of the product lineup and significant cost reductions were highlighted as completed actions rather than planned initiatives.
  • Guidance language shifted from caution and an emphasis on stabilization to a focus on sequential revenue improvement and targeted double-digit sales growth in the second half of 2026. Analysts' questions shifted from structural concerns to execution details around the product launch, margin expansion, and marketing spend.
  • Management sentiment improved, appearing more confident in turnaround execution, while analyst tone was less skeptical and more focused on operational specifics.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged liquidity and capital structure as ongoing challenges, indicating engagement with Guggenheim Securities to advise on refinancing efforts.
  • Early 2026 sales were impacted by severe weather and macroeconomic pressures, with 236 stores closed for at least one day in January and a significant sales decline at the start of the year.
  • The company faces margin pressure in Q1 due to hard stop clearance of legacy inventory, with management expecting some discounting and margin hits as they transition to the new product lineup.
  • Analysts probed for risks related to marketing spend efficiency, product clearance, and the pace of margin and revenue recovery.

Final Takeaway

Sleep Number’s management underscored the company’s rapid progress in its turnaround strategy, with a leaner cost structure, a reset product lineup, and strengthened marketing initiatives. Early sales from the new ComfortMode bed and robust attach rates provide confidence in future growth, and management projects double-digit sales growth in the second half of 2026. Liquidity and margin pressures remain, but proactive steps are in place to manage capital structure and support sequential revenue and EBITDA improvements throughout the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Petco skyrockets after guidance surprises to the upside
data.description:
Petco Health and Wellness Company (WOOF) shot up 35% in late Thursday morning trading after investors latched on to the retailer's adjusted EBITDA guidance for Q1 that came in ahead of expectations. The company sees Q1 adjusted EBITDA of $92M to $94M (midpoint $93M) vs. $90.3M consensus.

Despite the big rally, Jefferies upgraded Petco (WOOF) to a Buy rating and set a price target of $5.00 (+50% upside from the current price). "Petco enters F26 headed towards growth with liquidity and profitability concerns now in the rearview mirror. The breadth of initiatives planned do not rely on an improving macro and play to the retailer's strengths, giving us confidence they can work," updated analyst Kaumil Gajrawala. The firm thinks the market is still undervaluing the progress made so far by Petco (WOOF) and the self-help improvements to come.

RBC Capital Markets also kept a bullish stance on Petco (WOOF) following the update. "It's still very much a show-me story, but mgmt is building an execution track record, which seems to have investors in the early stages of warming up to it," highlighted analyst Steven Shemesh. RBC has an Outperform rating on WOOF and a price target of $4.00.
